name: Chicago
tagline: World-Class Architecture, Deep Dish, and a Stunning Lakefront — At Half the Coastal Price
description: Chicago is the FIRE retiree's secret weapon among major US cities — genuine big-city culture, dining, and architecture at roughly half the cost of New York or San Francisco. The lakefront rivals any coastal city, the food scene punches above its weight with more Michelin stars than LA, and neighborhoods like Logan Square and Wicker Park hum with creative energy. The trade-off is winter: brutal cold from December through March pushes up utility bills and tests your resolve. But those who stay are rewarded with magical summers and a city that fiercely earns its keep.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens. Illinois has a flat 4.95% state income tax — moderate compared to coastal states.
quickFacts.internetSpeed: 200-1000 Mbps fiber available via AT&T and Xfinity
quickFacts.safetyRating: Moderately Safe — crime varies dramatically by neighborhood; most residential areas are very safe
quickFacts.nearestAirport: O'Hare International Airport (ORD) — 35 min from downtown; Midway (MDW) — 25 min
quickFacts.climate: Humid continental with frigid winters (-10 to -1°C) and warm, humid summers (22-30°C). Lake effect moderates temperatures near the shore. Spectacular fall foliage.

housing.description: 1-bedroom apartment in Rogers Park, Uptown, or Pilsen — diverse neighborhoods with great food and CTA access

dining.description: Maxwell Street Polish sausages, jibarito sandwiches in Humboldt Park, dim sum in Chinatown, and deep dish at Lou Malnati's on special occasions

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace bronze plan through Get Covered Illinois, community health center for routine visits

transportation.description: CTA unlimited pass for the L train and buses — Chicago's transit system covers the city well

entertainment.description: Free days at the Art Institute and Field Museum, lakefront trail runs, neighborhood festivals, free summer concerts at Millennium Park

1M.lifestyle: Chicago's lower cost of living compared to coastal cities means $3,333 a month goes further here. You can get your own one-bedroom in Rogers Park or Pilsen, ride the L to free museum days, and eat well at neighborhood taquerias and dim sum spots. The lakefront trail is free year-round. Winter heating bills are a real budget item, and the cold from December through March is no joke, but summer in Chicago is genuinely rewarding.

housing.description: Spacious 1-bedroom or small 2-bedroom in Wicker Park, Logan Square, or Lakeview — trendy neighborhoods with walkable dining and nightlife

dining.description: Regular dinners at Girl and the Goat, Lula Cafe, and neighborhood BYOB gems. Weekend brunch in Logan Square, cocktails at speakeasies

healthcare.description: ACA silver plan with access to Northwestern Medicine or Rush University Medical Center, dental and vision included

transportation.description: CTA pass plus Divvy bike share membership, occasional Lyft for winter nights, weekend car rental for trips to Indiana Dunes

entertainment.description: Second City improv shows, Cubs games at Wrigley, Art Institute membership, live blues at Kingston Mines, architecture boat tours

2M.lifestyle: A comfortable apartment in Wicker Park, Logan Square, or Lakeview with walkable access to restaurants and nightlife. You eat out regularly at neighborhood spots, catch Cubs games at Wrigley, and take advantage of Chicago's deep cultural offerings including improv at Second City and live blues. Summer is the payoff -- beach volleyball at North Avenue, rooftop bars, and lakefront festivals. Illinois's 4.95% flat income tax is moderate compared to coastal states.

housing.description: Large 2-bedroom in Lincoln Park, a vintage condo in Gold Coast, or modern high-rise in the West Loop with skyline views

dining.description: Alinea tasting menus, regular Smyth and Oriole dinners, private wine pairings, chef's table experiences at Michelin-starred kitchens across the city

healthcare.description: Premium PPO with Northwestern or University of Chicago Medicine, concierge primary care, annual executive physicals

transportation.description: Leased car for winter convenience and weekend escapes, CTA for daily transit, occasional ride service

entertainment.description: Chicago Symphony Orchestra season tickets, Bears or Bulls premium seats, private sailing on Lake Michigan, Steppenwolf theater membership

3M.lifestyle: A large apartment in Lincoln Park or a vintage condo in the Gold Coast. You dine at top-tier restaurants like Alinea and Smyth regularly, hold season tickets to the Chicago Symphony, and sail on Lake Michigan in summer. At $10,000 a month, Chicago is genuinely affluent -- the city's lower cost of living compared to New York or SF means this budget buys a lifestyle that would cost significantly more on either coast.

housing.description: Luxury penthouse in the Gold Coast or Streeterville with lake and skyline views, or a renovated Lincoln Park greystone with private garden

dining.description: Private chef for weeknight dinners, regular Alinea and Ever experiences, exclusive wine dinners, hosted dinner parties with catering from top kitchens

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine through Northwestern Executive Health, international travel coverage, wellness coaching, full dental and vision

transportation.description: Leased luxury vehicle (BMW or Mercedes), private car service for events, weekend drives to Lake Geneva or Michigan wine country

entertainment.description: Private box at United Center, patron-level memberships at the Art Institute and CSO, private yacht charter on Lake Michigan, golf at Olympia Fields

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, weekly deep cleaning, snow removal and seasonal property maintenance

luxuryExtras.description: Personal trainer at East Bank Club, private art advisor, premium club membership, annual summer house rental in Lake Geneva

5M.lifestyle: A luxury penthouse in the Gold Coast with lake views, or a renovated Lincoln Park greystone with a private garden. You have a housekeeper, dine at the city's best restaurants without hesitation, and drive a luxury car for weekend trips to Lake Geneva or Michigan wine country. Business class flights for longer travel fit easily within the budget. At $16,667 a month, Chicago delivers an upper-affluent lifestyle that would cost far more in New York or San Francisco.

housing.description: Trophy residence on Lake Shore Drive with floor-to-ceiling lake views, or a historic Gold Coast mansion with carriage house and private garden

dining.description: Full-time private chef with Michelin training, curated wine cellar rivaling top restaurants, exclusive supper clubs, charity dinner hosting for 100

healthcare.description: Elite concierge medical team, longevity and anti-aging programs, fly-anywhere specialist access, private wellness retreats

transportation.description: Chauffeured luxury vehicle, NetJets fractional share for coast-to-coast travel, luxury car collection in the carriage house

entertainment.description: Board seats at the Art Institute and Lyric Opera, philanthropic galas, private art collection, courtside Bulls seats, wine estate in Michigan

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff: estate manager, private chef, housekeeper, personal assistant, driver, and seasonal groundskeeper

luxuryExtras.description: Interior designer on retainer, bespoke tailoring, charitable foundation management, private art curator, Lake Geneva estate maintenance

10M.lifestyle: A large home on Lake Shore Drive or a historic Gold Coast mansion. You have a full-time housekeeper, a cook who comes in regularly, and a personal assistant. You dine anywhere you want, travel business or first class, and support the Art Institute and Lyric Opera as a major donor. A second home in Lake Geneva or on the Michigan lakefront is feasible. Chicago's relative affordability means this budget goes very far -- you live like established wealth in a world-class city.
